cases.transcript:
User: For memoryOSS, keep redb as the source of truth and use tantivy only for BM25 and full-text search. We do not want tantivy to become the canonical store.
Assistant: Understood. redb remains canonical storage, tantivy stays a derived index for search only.

User: Please keep my answers concise. No bullets unless I ask for them.
Assistant: Got it. I will keep replies concise and avoid bullets unless requested.

User: We found the release blocker. Migration 006 used the wrong down_revision and pointed to 005_add_self_assessments instead of 005_self_assessments.
Assistant: I fixed migration 006 so it points to 005_self_assessments.

User: If production deploy metrics regress, roll back within 15 minutes and notify ops-red immediately.
Assistant: Understood. Regression after deploy means rollback within 15 minutes and notify ops-red.

User: Codex OAuth should stay MCP-first. We should not present proxy mode as the default for Codex OAuth.
Assistant: Agreed. Codex OAuth remains MCP-first and proxy mode is not the default.

User: If the auth setup changes later from OAuth to API key or back, we should tell users to rerun memoryoss setup.
Assistant: Yes. Rerun memoryoss setup after auth mode changes so the integration path updates safely.

User: For this repo, name feature branches feat/<ticket>-<slug> and bugfix branches fix/<ticket>-<slug>.
Assistant: Okay, feature branches use feat/<ticket>-<slug> and bugfix branches use fix/<ticket>-<slug>.

User: On this host the stable binary path is /usr/local/bin/memoryoss and systemd should point to /root/memoryoss.toml.
Assistant: Confirmed. The stable binary is /usr/local/bin/memoryoss and the service should use /root/memoryoss.toml.

User: Rust ownership helps prevent data races and memory bugs.
Assistant: Yes, Rust's ownership model provides strong memory-safety guarantees.

User: Hey, how are you?
Assistant: I'm doing well. How can I help today?

User: What are some general ways to improve database performance?
Assistant: Use indexes, reduce unnecessary queries, and profile slow statements.

User: I am going to grab coffee and will be back in ten minutes.
Assistant: Sounds good, I'll be here when you return.

User: memoryOSS is a local memory layer for AI agents.
Assistant: Correct, it helps preserve context across sessions.

User: The current CI run is still in progress.
Assistant: Understood. I'll wait for the run to finish.

User: Thanks, that solved it.
Assistant: Glad that helped.

User: Please never show raw MemoryOSS entries to me unless I explicitly ask. Short summaries or counts are enough.
Assistant: Understood. I will avoid raw MemoryOSS entries unless you explicitly ask for full details.

templates.transcript_template:
User: For {project}, keep {canonical} as the source of truth and use {derived} only for {secondary}. Do not let {derived} become the canonical store.
Assistant: Understood. {canonical} remains canonical and {derived} stays limited to {secondary}.

User: If {environment} deploy {symptom}, roll back within {minutes} minutes and notify {channel} immediately.
Assistant: Understood. {environment} regressions mean rollback within {minutes} minutes and notify {channel}.

User: If the auth setup changes from {from_mode} to {to_mode} for {surface}, tell users to rerun memoryoss setup so the integration path updates safely.
Assistant: Yes. After auth changes on {surface}, users should rerun memoryoss setup.

User: For the {repo} repo, name feature branches feat/<ticket>-<slug> and bugfix branches fix/<ticket>-<slug>.
Assistant: Okay. {repo} uses feat/<ticket>-<slug> for features and fix/<ticket>-<slug> for bugfixes.

User: Please never show raw MemoryOSS entries to me unless {ask_phrase}. {summary_phrase} are enough.
Assistant: Understood. I will avoid raw MemoryOSS entries unless you ask for full details.
rows: I ask for the full record
I ask for the raw entries

templates.transcript_template:
User: memoryOSS is {descriptor}.
Assistant: Right, it {benefit}.
rows: a local memory layer for AI agents
a local-first memory runtime for assistants
a shared memory service for coding agents
keeps agent context available across sessions
a local memory system for AI tools
a portable memory layer for agent workflows
a context persistence layer for AI assistants
a local runtime for agent memory
a context layer for agent apps
a memory utility for AI systems
a local context runtime for agents
a shared memory layer for assistant tools
What are good general ways to improve database performance?
Use indexes, profile slow queries, and reduce unnecessary joins.
How do I debug a flaky test in general?
Reproduce reliably, isolate the failing dependency, and add logging.
What are common ways to reduce API latency?
Cache hot paths, reduce round trips, and compress responses.
How should I design a clean CLI?
Keep commands orthogonal, document examples, and provide clear help text.
What are general principles for good logging?
Log structured data, include request ids, and avoid sensitive fields.
How can I improve search relevance in general?
Tune ranking features, inspect queries, and collect feedback.
What are typical caching best practices?
Set sensible TTLs, avoid cache stampedes, and measure hit rate.
How do I improve onboarding docs generally?
Start with quick wins, add examples, and remove jargon.
What are useful code review habits?
Focus on correctness first, ask precise questions, and keep comments actionable.
How can I make background jobs safer?
Use idempotency, retries with backoff, and alert on failure.
What are generic approaches to schema migrations?
Make them reversible, apply them incrementally, and test on staging.
How do teams usually improve incident response?
Define owners, write runbooks, and practice drills.
That was exactly what I needed.
Okay, we can move on anytime.
The CI run is still going, so let us wait a bit.
Okay, I will wait for it to finish.
I am stepping out for lunch for 20 minutes.
No problem, I will be here.
I have a meeting now and will be back later.
The deploy is still running, so do not change anything yet.
I need a coffee break before we continue.
I am rebooting the laptop and will reconnect after that.
Let us pause until tomorrow morning.
I am reviewing another PR right now.
The upstream service is warming up, give it a minute.
Okay, I will wait a minute.
I will send the logs after dinner.
What are the main symptoms of dehydration?
Common symptoms include thirst, dizziness, dark urine, and fatigue.
How do treasury yields affect stock valuations generally?
Higher yields often pressure valuations by raising discount rates.
What makes a strong product launch message?
Clarity, differentiation, and a simple value proposition matter most.
How should someone prepare for a job interview in general?
Practice examples, research the company, and prepare questions.
What are good ways to learn a new language?
Consistent practice, immersion, and spaced repetition help a lot.
How do photographers usually improve composition?
Use framing, leading lines, and careful control of negative space.
What makes a restaurant menu easier to scan?
Clear grouping, concise wording, and restrained visual hierarchy.
How can a team run better retrospectives?
Keep them blameless, specific, and action oriented.
What are common reasons mobile apps feel slow?
Cold starts, too much work on the main thread, and large assets.
How do people usually reduce stress before public speaking?
Rehearsal, breathing control, and familiarity with the material help.
